events.title: Explosion and fire during fuel loading at Chevron Refinery delivery pier (Barge TTT-103), Pascagoula, Mississippi
events.description: An explosion and subsequent fire occurred during loading operations involving a barge at the Chevron Refinery delivery pier in Pascagoula, Mississippi. Responders including the U.S. Coast Guard and a strike team were involved due to potential oil pollution risk.

sources.headline: Explosion and fire during fuel loading at Chevron Refinery delivery pier (Barge TTT-103), Pascagoula, Mississippi

key_quotes.quote: a major fire involved the barge and pier; U.S. Coast Guard and Gulf Strike Team responded

events.title: Chlorine-related warehouse fire and vapor plume near Jones Chemical, St. Petersburg, Florida
events.description: A warehouse fire at Jones Chemical in St. Petersburg, Florida reportedly produced a downwind vapor plume containing chlorine and hydrochloric acid vapors. Evacuations were conducted for residents and businesses downwind of the facility.

sources.headline: Chlorine-related warehouse fire and vapor plume; evacuations near Jones Chemical, St. Petersburg, Florida

key_quotes.quote: produced a large downwind vapor plume reportedly containing chlorine and hydrochloric acid vapors

events.title: Chlorine leak discovered in rail car at Rigby Yard, South Portland, Maine
events.description: A leak was discovered in a rail car containing chlorine at Rigby Yard in South Portland, Maine. The rail car was segregated and responders including the fire department and industry representatives attended the incident.

sources.headline: Leak discovered in rail car containing chlorine at Rigby Yard (Maine Central Railroad), South Portland, Maine

key_quotes.quote: a leak was discovered in a rail car containing chlorine at Rigby Yard

events.title: Sodium cyanide spill on Interstate 195 East near Providence, Rhode Island
events.description: Multiple containers of sodium cyanide spilled onto Interstate 195 East near Providence, Rhode Island. State environmental officials and the Providence Fire Department responded to manage the hazardous materials incident.

sources.headline: Sodium cyanide spill on Interstate 195 East near Providence, Rhode Island

key_quotes.quote: eight 100-pound containers of sodium cyanide spilled onto Interstate 195 East near Providence

events.title: Styrene release into Philadelphia sewer system from Ashland Chemical Corporation, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
events.description: Ashland Chemical Corporation inadvertently released an estimated 170 gallons of styrene into the Philadelphia sewer system. The incident was reported as occurring around midnight on August 13, 1986.

sources.headline: Styrene release into Philadelphia sewer system (Ashland Chemical Corporation), Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

key_quotes.quote: inadvertently released about 170 gallons of styrene into the Philadelphia sewer system

events.title: Widespread floods reported across China; at least 233 deaths
events.description: A news report said widespread flooding affected multiple provinces in China, including reported impacts in Yunnan and mentions of Jilin, Hubei, Guangdong, and Guangxi. The report stated at least 233 people were killed and described large-scale impacts to housing and cropland.

sources.headline: Floods in China kill at least 233

key_quotes.quote: Floods in China kill at least 233

events.title: Typhoon-related heavy rain and flooding reported in Guangxi, China
events.description: A news report said a typhoon brought very heavy rain to Guangxi Province, with flooding reported in agricultural areas and damage to crops and homes. The report described disrupted communications in some places and homes swept away by flooding.

sources.headline: Typhoon floods china rice paddies

events.title: Hurricane Charley brings flooding rain and high tides to North Carolina Outer Banks
events.description: NOAA materials describing Hurricane Charley (1986) report that the storm affected North Carolina’s Outer Banks with heavy rainfall and coastal flooding from high tides. Impacts included flooded roads and other flooding-related disruptions along parts of the Outer Banks.

location.name: Raleigh, North Carolina, United States (proxy for North Carolina Outer Banks impacts)

sources.headline: Hurricane Charley (1986) Review
Hurricane Charley (1986) Rainfall Summary

events.title: Typhoon Wayne strikes Taiwan with damaging winds and heavy rain
events.description: Two news reports said Typhoon Wayne hit Taiwan, bringing strong winds and heavy rain that caused widespread damage and casualties. The reports described damage to infrastructure and housing associated with the storm’s impacts.

sources.headline: Typhoon Wayne kills 22 injures 110
Typhoon Wayne kills 39 on Taiwan

events.title: Typhoon Wayne reported to strike Taiwan a second time after reversing course
events.description: A news report said Typhoon Wayne reversed course after earlier impacts and affected Taiwan again, with the storm center passing near the island’s southern tip. The report described strong winds affecting southern portions of Taiwan during the second strike.

sources.headline: Typhoon Wayne makes second strike at Taiwan

key_quotes.quote: Typhoon Wayne makes second strike at Taiwan

events.title: Intermediate-depth Vrancea-region earthquake impacts central Romania (Aug 30, 1986)
events.description: An intermediate-depth earthquake occurred in Romania’s Vrancea seismic region on August 30, 1986. Sources describe damage impacts in central Romania and document the event as part of late July–August 1986 seismicity reporting.

sources.headline: Earthquakes July-August 1986

events.title: Anderson Fire in Boise National Forest declared contained
events.description: Officials reported the roughly 18,000-acre Anderson fire in Idaho as the last major blaze in the West and said it was declared contained on Aug. 28, 1986. The report situates the fire amid widespread August wildfires across the western United States.

location.name: Boise National Forest near Crouch, Idaho, USA

sources.headline: Last major fire in West contained

key_quotes.quote: Last major fire in West contained

events.title: Anderson Fire described as last major blaze still raging in the West
events.description: A report said about 3,000 firefighters were working on an approximately 18,000-acre fire in Idaho’s Boise National Forest. The fire was described as the only major blaze still burning after lightning-triggered wildfires across multiple western U.S. states.

sources.headline: About 3,000 firefighters struggled today with an 18,000-acre blaze

key_quotes.quote: About 3,000 firefighters struggled today with an 18,000-acre blaze

events.title: Fire spreads in Cuyamaca Rancho State Park with closures and evacuations
events.description: Los Angeles Times coverage reported a wildfire in Cuyamaca Rancho State Park in San Diego County that grew to hundreds of acres and prompted closures and evacuations of campers. The articles describe firefighting efforts and partial containment around Aug. 21–22, 1986.

location.name: Cuyamaca Rancho State Park, San Diego County, California, USA

sources.headline: (LA Times archive story, Aug. 22, 1986)
(LA Times archive story, Aug. 21, 1986)

events.title: Cuyamaca Rancho State Park fire reported at about 1,097 acres and 90% contained
events.description: A Los Angeles Times report said a three-day wildfire in Cuyamaca Rancho State Park reached about 1,097 acres. The story reported roughly 90% containment after burnout operations.

location.name: Cuyamaca Rancho State Park, San Diego County, California, USA

sources.headline: (LA Times archive story, Aug. 23, 1986)

events.title: Small brush fire near Delta Flats damages buildings in Angeles National Forest
events.description: A Los Angeles Times report described a brush fire near Delta Flats in the Angeles National Forest that burned about four acres. The fire destroyed three storage buildings and a car and damaged a garage before firefighters surrounded the blaze.

location.name: Near Delta Flats / Big Tujunga Canyon Road, Angeles National Forest, California, USA

sources.headline: (LA Times archive story, Aug. 25, 1986)

events.title: U.S. EPA issues technical correction to the RCRA Final Codification Rule
events.description: EPA published a Federal Register notice (51 FR 28556) making a technical correction to the July 15, 1985 Final Codification Rule under RCRA/HSWA. The notice addresses corrections related to codification and reporting applicability described in the EPA summary.

sources.headline: Federal Register Notice: Technical Correction to Final Codification Rule

events.title: U.S. EPA publishes final rule and correction for hazardous waste tank systems standards under RCRA
events.description: EPA published a Federal Register final rule and correction (51 FR 29430) establishing standards for hazardous waste storage and treatment tank systems under RCRA. The notice also corrects errors described by EPA in relation to the tank systems rule publication.

sources.headline: Federal Register Notice: Correction to Final Rule: Standards for Hazardous Waste Storage and Treatment Tank Systems

events.title: U.S. EPA corrects hazardous waste listing information for commercial chemical products and Appendix VIII constituents
events.description: EPA issued a correction dated August 6, 1986 related to hazardous waste identification/listing information affecting commercial chemical products and Appendix VIII constituents under RCRA. The draft source references this item via EPA's state authorization rule checklist index (Revision Checklist 29).

sources.headline: State Authorization Rule Checklists (Hazardous Waste Rulemakings)

events.title: U.S. EPA promulgates final rule governing exports of hazardous waste (RCRA)
events.description: EPA promulgated a final rule (51 FR 28664, dated August 8, 1986) establishing requirements applicable to exports of hazardous waste. The rule includes advance notification and reporting requirements for exporters as summarized in the EPA notice.

sources.headline: Hazardous Waste Management System; Exports of Hazardous Waste; Final Rule (Federal Register Notice)
